title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs dependency graphs, incremental builds, caches, and watch mode from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Clean Equivalence
steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 3290 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.

Inspect the Clean Equivalence trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Clean Equivalence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences. For Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Clean Equivalence, keep Clean Equivalence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Clean Equivalence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Clean Equivalence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 5623 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare incremental and clean route manifests, bytes, diagnostics, removals, and digests across randomized change sequences.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Clean Equivalence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Clean Equivalence, keep Clean Equivalence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
